plainMeaning: Diploma nostrification — the procedure that establishes a foreign diploma's Polish equivalent when no treaty already recognizes it automatically. Whether it applies to you depends on the profession: for regulated professions (for example doctor, nurse, architect), recognizing the diploma is generally required before you can practice, regardless of nationality. For most other, non-regulated jobs, there is no government recognition step — the employer decides directly whether to accept a foreign diploma. NAWA's KWALIFIKATOR database can help you check how a specific foreign diploma compares, but using it is not obligatory.
